caption:
Five months ago I watched this Alex Hormozi video where he said 80% of your effort should be focused on the first 5 seconds. 

And it was after that moment that I started really studying hooks from a creator POV to understand them. So that’s what I recommend for you, too. 

Also, hooks don’t always have to be “aggressive”. 

For example, vulnerability hooks like “I’m X age and I’m a bit ashamed to admit that ____” is one that popped off in the past. Just create that open loop.

If you want my free guide on how I’d speedrun 10k followers just comment SPEED and I’ll send it to you.

caption:
This is How To Get 3D Tracking Title Cards On Your Phone!

Comment the word “BANANA” 🍌 to get my 3D reference pack and prompts

Here’s how you do it 👇

1️⃣ Film a video of you holding an object.
Screenshot the first frame of the video.

2️⃣ Head to Higgsfield.ai → Create → Images → Nano Banana Pro.
Upload your screenshot and one of the reference images from my 3D reference pack.

3️⃣ Paste this prompt and customise the text however you like.

Create a 3D text anchored to the [object] in the image.
Use the text graphic from the camera photo as reference.
The text should say [your custom title].

4️⃣ Choose 4 generations and hit generate.
Download your favourite result.

5️⃣ To animate it, go to Create → Videos → Create Video.
Change the model to Kling 3.0 Omni.
Upload the plain screenshot as the start frame and the generated image as the end frame.
Paste in the animation prompt and generate.

6️⃣ To track the text, go to Kling 3.0 Omni Edit.
Upload your full video, along with the plain screenshot and the generated image.

7️⃣ Paste in the tracking prompt and hit generate.

Voila!
You now have 3D tracking title cards with no VFX experience needed.

Like and follow for more!
@higgsfield.ai

caption:
REMINDER 10/30: Fastest Way to Kill Your Storytelling (Part 02) 👉DM “Blueprint” for Free Guide

Most people think storytelling frameworks are just tools for writers and content creators. They’re not.

They’re blueprints for guiding human emotion toward an outcome.

Every effective framework does three things:
→ Makes them see themselves as the protagonist
→ Shows them the friction they’re experiencing
→ Guides them to the resolution

This isn’t just about better content. It’s about influence.

Getting the job. Earning the raise. Pitching investors. Building stronger relationships. Resolving conflict. Moving people from where they are to where you need them to be.

You study the framework to write better stories. But understanding WHY it works? That’s the key to influence beyond content creation.

🔥Want the full breakdown?
👉DM me “Blueprint” for my FREE guide to learn all the major frameworks and how to apply them to every part of your life.

👉Drop a Comment: What’s one area outside of content where you want to apply this?

#progressoverperfection #personalbranding #storytelling #contentcreation #personalgrowth

caption:
Save this and steal a hook before your next reel.

Most hooks fail for one reason.

They sound like titles.
Not like tension.

In 2026, the hook is not a sentence.
It’s a trigger that makes someone think: “Wait… that’s me.”

That’s why this carousel works:
It shows the upgrade from weak, generic hooks
to hooks that create curiosity, urgency, and identity.

Use these 12 hook patterns when your content feels flat:
Learning curve, pain-avoidance, transformation, mistake, hidden truth, authority, effort vs result, brutal truth, shortcut revelation, rebuild from zero, before it’s too late, if I could go back.

One pattern per post.
One emotion per hook.
One clear outcome.

[Hook Writing, Viral Hooks 2026, Instagram Growth 2026, Reels Hooks, Copywriting Hooks, Content Strategy, Storytelling Hooks, Attention Hooks, Social Media Marketing, Creator Growth, Personal Branding, Persuasive Writing, Content That Converts, Short Form Content, Sorav Jain, Ai Marketer, Ai Coach, Ai expert]

latestComments.text: Love this perspective. Hooks should trigger curiosity, not just sound like titles.
Love the ‘hook as tension’ idea. I’ve noticed when the hook reflects a real problem the audience feels, retention jumps instantly
